text.title: AI Orphan-Link Detection Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules with rationale + source for ai-orphan-link-detection. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Every relative link MUST resolve to a file present in the repo at PR HEAD; broken paths are orphans.
rule.rationale: Markdown link-check tools (markdown-link-check, lychee) — broken relatives are the most common orphan class.

rule.statement: Every `#fragment` link MUST match a heading slug present in the target file at PR HEAD.
rule.rationale: Anchors drift silently when headings are renamed; CI is the only gate.

rule.statement: `[[slug]]` style methodology cross-links MUST appear in the canonical slug index (knowledge L2 INDEX.xml union).
rule.rationale: Faion methodology cross-links use `[[slug]]` shorthand; agents hallucinate slugs.

rule.statement: External http(s) links MUST return non-4xx on a HEAD request. Throttle to 1 req/host/sec to avoid flagging rate-limit responses as 404.
rule.rationale: Cloudflare / Akamai often return 4xx to bot-like HEADs; throttle + retry distinguishes real 404 from rate-limit.

rule.statement: Links pointing to files marked `status: deprecated` MUST be flagged with severity=medium even if the file exists.
rule.rationale: Deprecated targets rot; failing forward beats failing later.

rule.statement: If the markdown is a personal scratchpad with no consumers, or all links are external (use a different tool), return `skipped`.
rule.rationale: Avoids false positives on non-doc content.
